revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町]]入田136 |位置= |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]] |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった岡山城址に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は[[淳仁天皇]]の御内室「室妃」で、[[奈良時代]]の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で[[淡路]]へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる<ref>{{cite web|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/murohime_title.htm|title=室姫神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-5-10}}</ref>。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-4-12}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[新野駅]]」より車で約5分。
== 脚注 == <references />
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むろひめしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

{{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]入田136 |位置= |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]] |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |}} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった岡山城址に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。

室比賣神社として祀るようになり、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-4-12}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]]「[[新野駅]]」より車で約5分。

[[File:Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg|thumb|250px]]
== 歴史 == 中世にあった岡山城址に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。

{{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]入田136 | 緯度度 = 33| 緯度分 = 51| 緯度秒 = 02.4 | 経度度 = 134| 経度分 = 35| 経度秒 = 31.9 |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]] |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]にある[[神社]]である。'''室姫神社'''とも称される。
[[File:Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg|thumb|250px]]
== 概要 == 境内には[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城址]]がある。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城]]に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は[[淳仁天皇]]の御内室「室妃」で、[[奈良時代]]の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で[[淡路島|淡路]]へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる<ref>{{cite web|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/murohime_title.htm|title=室姫神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-5-10}}</ref>。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-4-12}}</ref>。

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}

{{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]] |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]にある[[神社]]。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城]]に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は[[淳仁天皇]]の御内室「室妃」で、[[奈良時代]]の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で[[淡路島|淡路]]へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/murohime_title.htm|title=室姫神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-10}}</ref>。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-04-12}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]]
== 境内 == 境内は[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城址]]となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[新野駅 (徳島県)|新野駅]]より車で約5分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:むろひめしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿南市の建築物]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|室比賣神社}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

<!--generated by generate_shikinaisha_pages_v25_with_redirects.py--> {{Expand Japanese|室比賣神社}}
{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

revision.comment: 20 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]] |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]にある[[神社]]。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城]]に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は[[淳仁天皇]]の御内室「室妃」で、[[奈良時代]]の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で[[淡路島|淡路]]へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/murohime_title.htm|title=室姫神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-10}}</ref>。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-04-12}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]]
== 境内 == 境内は[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城址]]となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[新野駅 (徳島県)|新野駅]]より車で約5分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:むろひめしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿南市の建築物]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]]
{{translated page|ja|室比賣神社|version=107352806|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]] |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[新野町 (阿南市)|新野町]]にある[[神社]]。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城]]に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は[[淳仁天皇]]の御内室「室妃」で、[[奈良時代]]の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で[[淡路島|淡路]]へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/murohime_title.htm|title=室姫神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-10}}</ref>。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、[[1874年]]（[[明治]]7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-04-12}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|室比賣命]]
== 境内 == 境内は[[岡山城 (阿波国)|岡山城址]]となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[新野駅 (徳島県)|新野駅]]より車で約5分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}
[[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿南市の建築物]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]]
{{translated page|ja|室比賣神社|version=107352806|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}} |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
== 歴史 == 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://genbu.net/data/awa2/murohime_title.htm|title=室姫神社|publisher=玄松子の記憶|accessdate=2021-05-10}}</ref>。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.komainu.org/tokusima/anansi/MurohimeAratano/murohime.html|title=室比賣神社|publisher=延喜式神社の調査|accessdate=2021-04-12}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
== 境内 == 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
== 交通 == * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}}
[[category:徳島県の神社]]
==End Japanese== [[Category:阿南市の建築物]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]]

== street address (P6375) ==
* {'text': '徳島県阿南市新野町入田136', 'language': 'ja'}
[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}} |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
=== 歴史 === 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称。
=== 祭神 === * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
=== 境内 === 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。
=== 脚注 === {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].
== instance of (P31) ==

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}} |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
=== 歴史 === 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称。
=== 祭神 === * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
=== 境内 === 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。
=== 脚注 === {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

=== 歴史 === 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称。
=== 祭神 === * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
=== 境内 === 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== street address (P6375) ==
* {'text': '徳島県阿南市新野町入田136', 'language': 'ja'}
[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}} |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
=== 歴史 === 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称。
=== 祭神 === * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
=== 境内 === 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= 室比賣神社（室姫神社） |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}入田136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}} |社格= 旧無格社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''室比賣神社'''（むろひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿南市|Q485583}}{{jalink|新野町 (阿南市)|Q28686788|新野町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。'''室姫神社'''とも表記される。
=== 歴史 === 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称。
=== 祭神 === * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
=== 境内 === 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

=== 歴史 === 中世にあった{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城}}に鎮座。地図や神額等では'''室姫神社'''と表記されるが、徳島県神社庁では室比賣神社と記載されている。
当社の祭神は{{jalink|淳仁天皇|Q329713}}の御内室「室妃」で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の末期に、姫が天皇を慕って小舟で{{jalink|淡路島|Q504373|淡路}}へ向かう途中、当地に漂着し、その後、祀ったとされる。
室比賣神社として祀るようになり、{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年）には室姫神社と改称。
=== 祭神 === * {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|室比賣命}}
=== 境内 === 境内は{{jalink|岡山城 (阿波国)||岡山城址}}となっている。
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|牟岐線|Q860478}}{{jalink|新野駅 (徳島県)|Q4784417|新野駅}}より車で約5分。

== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}{{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}}, {{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{jalink|Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|Aratano Station}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}.

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}{{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}}, {{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{jalink|Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|Aratano Station}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}.

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}{{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}}, {{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{jalink|Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|Aratano Station}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}.

=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}{{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}}, {{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{jalink|Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|Aratano Station}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}.

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}{{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}}, {{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{jalink|Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|Aratano Station}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}{{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|Aratano-chō}}, {{jalink|Anan, Tokushima|Q485583}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{jalink|Emperor Junnin|Q329713}}. At the end of the {{jalink|Nara period|Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{jalink|Awaji Island|Q504373|Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{jalink|Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{jalink|Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|Aratano Station}} on the {{jalink|Mugi Line|Q860478}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531|JR}}.

At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)||WD=|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.

=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}.

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}.

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}}.
=== Notes === {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

[[JA:室比賣神社]]
{{wikidata link|Q106852500}}
== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

== Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称= Murohime Shrine (Murohime Jinja) |画像= [[File:Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}} Iruta 136 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |社格= former unranked shrine |創建= unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=室比賣神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Murohime Shrine''' (むろひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Aratano-chō (Anan)|Q28686788|WD=Q28686788|lt=Aratano-chō|1=Aratanochō}}, {{ill|lt=Anan, Tokushima|Q485583|WD=Q485583|1=Anan, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is also written as '''Murohime Jinja'''.
=== History === It was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle}} in the medieval period. On maps and shrine plaques it is written as '''Murohime Jinja''', but the Tokushima Prefecture Shrine Office lists it as Murohime Shrine.
The enshrined deity is "Murohi," the consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Junnin|Q329713|WD=Q329713|1=Emperor Junnin}}. At the end of the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, she is said to have longed for the emperor and set out for {{ill|lt=Awaji Island|Q504373|WD=Q504373|lt=Awaji|1=Awaji Island}} in a small boat, drifted ashore at this location, and was later enshrined here.
After being enshrined as Murohime Shrine, it was renamed Murohime Jinja in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} (Meiji 7).
=== Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Murohime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
=== Precincts === The shrine precincts are at the {{ill|lt=Okayama Castle (Awa Province)|UNKNOWN|lt=Okayama Castle ruins}}.
<gallery> Aratano-okayamajo-castle.jpg Aratano-okayamajo-castle 2.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 3.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 4.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 5.jpg Aratano-murohime-shrine 6.jpg </gallery>
=== Access === * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Aratano Station (Tokushima)|Q4784417|WD=Q4784417|lt=Aratano Station|1=Aratano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Mugi Line|Q860478|WD=Q860478|1=Mugi Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|lt=JR|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Murohime Shrine | native_name = 室比賣神社 | image = Aratano-murohime-shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Murohime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.85066666666667|134.59219444444443}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Murohime Shrine'''|室比賣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].
